name: Borderlands Loop from Pinilla de Jadraque
name_original: De Medranda a Fuencemillán — circular desde Pinilla de Jadraque
description: This moderate 38.7-mile road cycling route from Pinilla de Jadraque offers historical landmarks and scenic views of the Guadalajara province
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pinilla De Jadraque traverse a landscape characterized by open countryside, rolling hills, and varying elevations. The region offers a mix of quiet roads connecting small villages and more challenging climbs. Road cyclists can expect routes that navigate through agricultural areas and provide views of the surrounding terrain. Best road cycling routes around Pinilla De Jadraque The most popular road cycling route is Borderlands Loop from Pinilla de Jadraque, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain with over 2,200 feet of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto Rey Climb via Villares de Jadraque, a difficult 25.0 miles (40.2 km) path. This route includes a significant ascent, gaining over 3,400 feet in elevation. Local road cyclists also love the View of Atienza Castle loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque, a 35.9 miles (57.8 km) trail leading through open landscapes with views of Atienza Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Pinilla De Jadraque is defined by open countryside, rolling hills, and routes connecting historic villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Pinilla De Jadraque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pinilla De Jadraque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: The region offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, characterized by open countryside, rolling hills, and varying elevations. You'll find quiet roads connecting small villages, routes through agricultural areas, and more challenging climbs. The network includes options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult ascents.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: While the routes around Pinilla De Jadraque are generally rated moderate to difficult, there are options that might be more approachable for beginners looking for a challenge. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375734695/borderlands-loop-from-pinilla-de-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borderlands Loop from Pinilla de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; are rated moderate and offer varied terrain with significant elevation gain, providing a good step up for those ready to advance.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: Advanced riders will find several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017172610/alto-rey-climb-via-villares-de-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Climb via Villares de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.2 km path with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle – Hiendelaencina loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 100 km with significant climbing.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle – Hiendelaencina loop from Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; cover over 100 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita – Jadraque Castle loop from Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; is another substantial option at over 85 km, offering a full day's adventure.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375734695/borderlands-loop-from-pinilla-de-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borderlands Loop from Pinilla de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: The routes offer views of open landscapes and connect historic villages. You might pass by notable landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6594742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Juan Bautista of Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; or catch glimpses of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4681084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt;. Several routes also provide views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region's rolling hills and open countryside naturally offer many scenic vistas. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Atienza Castle loop from Pálmaces de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; are specifically highlighted for their views of historic landmarks. You'll also encounter picturesque villages and expansive agricultural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: The best time for road cycling typically depends on personal preference for weather. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides ideal, while winters might be cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling on clear days.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet roads, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to explore charming small villages and historic sites while enjoying the open countryside.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Pinilla De Jadraque, offering a good selection for various skill levels and preferences. These include a mix of moderate loops and more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of the region's reservoirs. For example, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pálmaces Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable feature in the wider area.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Pinilla De Jadraque?
A: Many routes start from or pass through villages like Pinilla de Jadraque, Pálmaces de Jadraque, or Jadraque. You can typically find public parking in these villages, often near the town center or local amenities, which serve as convenient starting points for your rides.
tips: 10th century bridge over the Henares River created during the Caliphate of Guadalajara for the passage of trade to Zaragoza. Today the river landscape can provide you with shade and a place to rest on your journey.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road bike route is approximately 62.3 kilometers (38.7 miles) long. On average, cyclists can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 52 minutes, depending on their pace and stops at points of interest.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Pinilla de Jadraque?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Pinilla de Jadraque. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you should be able to find suitable parking within the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for racebikes and is classified as 'moderate' difficulty. It features an elevation profile with a highest point of 1,040 meters and a lowest point of 750 meters, involving climbs and descents typical for an average road bike ride in the region. The road surface quality is generally suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and scenic viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass by the 10th-century Stone Bridge over the Henares River, enjoy panoramic View of Cogolludo, and take in the View of Veguillas. The route also skirts the Alcorlo Reservoir, offering impressive vistas of the dam. Additionally, you'll encounter the Romanesque church of La Anunciación in Pinilla de Jadraque and the romantic ruins of the San Salvador monastery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot in Guadalajara province, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though specific seasonal weather details are not available.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as 'moderate' and described as an 'average road bike ride'. While not overly challenging, the 62.3 km distance and 685 meters of elevation gain mean it's best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You will find parts of the Tierras de Frontera, Camino del Cid (Senderista), GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, and various segments of the Camino del Cid.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several villages, including Medranda, Fuencemillán, and Pinilla de Jadraque. These villages may offer local cafes, pubs, or guesthouses, providing opportunities for refreshments or overnight stays. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists directly on the road for safety reasons. If you plan to visit the villages or stop at viewpoints, dogs may be allowed in public areas, but leash rules and local regulations should always be observed.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this public road route in the province of Guadalajara, Spain. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What makes this route particularly interesting for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for road cyclists who appreciate combining their ride with cultural and historical exploration. Beyond the physical challenge, it offers unique points of interest like ancient bridges, Romanesque architecture, and medieval monastery ruins, alongside diverse natural scenery from river landscapes to reservoir vistas and charming villages.

waypoints.name: Stone Bridge over the Henares River
